mission.title: knative: Knative options to support for shared GPU resources with PSP
mission.description: Knative options to support for shared GPU resources with PSP. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## Ask your question here:

In our use cases enabling GPU resource sharing would add huge value for running serverless GPU workloads as many of the workloads only occupies fraction of the GPU.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Knative options to support for shared GPU resources with PSP
steps.description:
Couple things to note here: with the move to Pod Security Standards in Kubernetes v1.25+, sharing host namespaces would be disallowed under non-"privileged" policies. It could also run into issues if we were to enable user namespaces in the future. 

That said, it's probably worth discussing whether this is something to consider enabling (behind a feature gate).  and  for any API or security concerns.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Knative options to support for shared GPU resources with PSP" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Couple things to note here: with the move to Pod Security Standards in Kubernetes v1.25+, sharing host namespaces would be disallowed under non-"privileged" policies. It could also run into issues if we were to enable user namespaces in the future. 

That said, it's probably worth discussing whether this is something to consider enabling (behind a feature gate).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
